close_reason: Simplified .envrc docs to use turnkey direnv library
description:
The .envrc snippet in docs/user-manual/src/getting-started/project-setup.md is overly complicated with manual symlink management.

**Current (complex):**
```bash
use flake . --no-pure-eval

# 20+ lines of manual symlink sync logic...
if [ -n "$TURNKEY_BUCK2_CONFIG" ]; then
  # ...
fi
for var in $(env | grep '^TURNKEY_CELL_' | cut -d= -f1); do
  # ...
done
```

**Should be (simple):**
```bash
use flake . --no-pure-eval

source "$TURNKEY_DIRENV_LIB"
use_turnkey
```

The turnkey direnv library ($TURNKEY_DIRENV_LIB) already handles all the symlink management, watch_file declarations, and optional regeneration. The documentation should use this simpler approach.

See the actual .envrc in this repository for the correct pattern.
title: Simplify .envrc documentation in project-setup.md
